history.qwen_observation: The aspectj-maven-plugin:1.9 fails to resolve com.sun:tools:jar on JDK 11 because tools.jar was removed in JDK 11+.
history.qwen_rationale: Upgrading aspectj-maven-plugin to 1.14.0 (which supports JDK 11+ and no longer depends on tools.jar) before the JDK 11 step will allow the build to succeed under JDK 11 and subsequent steps.

history.qwen_observation: AJC compiler fails on JDK 11 with 'java.lang.String cannot be resolved' due to an outdated AspectJ compiler version incompatible with the JDK 11+ module system.
history.qwen_rationale: Forcing the AspectJ tools/weaver/runtime to 1.9.20 and setting the aspectj.version property ensures the AJC compiler understands JDK 11+ class files, resolving the indirect reference error before the Java 11 migration step runs.

history.qwen_observation: AspectJ 1.9.20 and aspectj-maven-plugin 1.14.0 require a Java 11+ runtime, causing UnsupportedClassVersionError when executed under JDK 8.
history.qwen_rationale: Moving the aspectj_bump step (including the plugin upgrade) to JDK 11 satisfies the runtime requirement of the upgraded AspectJ toolchain. The project source remains Java 8-compatible, so mvn compile will succeed under JDK 11 before the explicit Java 8-to-11 migration step runs.

history.qwen_observation: The aspectj-maven-plugin 1.9 fails on JDK 11 because it attempts to load the removed tools.jar, and the plugin version upgrade recipe did not take effect during the build.
history.qwen_rationale: AspectJ 1.9.x is incompatible with JDK 11+ due to the removal of tools.jar. Upgrading to 1.14.0 resolves this. The previous recipe likely missed a property-managed version or pluginManagement declaration, so adding explicit ChangePropertyValue steps for common AspectJ plugin version properties ensures the POM is correctly updated before mvn compile runs under JDK 11.

history.qwen_observation: mvn rewrite:run failed on JDK 11 because the old aspectj-maven-plugin:1.9 cannot resolve com.sun:tools on JDK 11+, blocking recipe application and compilation.
history.qwen_rationale: Switching aspectj_bump to a POM_PATCH step allows direct XML patching of plugin and dependency versions before Maven attempts resolution. Forcing aspectj-maven-plugin to 1.14.0 and AspectJ libraries to 1.9.20 bypasses the tools.jar resolution failure, enabling the build to proceed to Java 11+ migration steps.
